cadence.tier2: weekly, cluster chosen by day of month

pipelines.A: Tenders / vendor RFPs: financial-sector training, curriculum, ToT, capacity building, institutional strengthening, evaluation/MEL, technical assistance
pipelines.B: Philanthropic RFPs / grant calls: funding or services for nonprofits, think tanks, conveners of institutional investors and capital
pipelines.C: Broad development (OPEN calls only): anything broadly fitting bringing capital to Africa, long-term sustainable development in any sector, or human capital programs

tier1.name: @AfricanNGOs monthly roundup
tier1.protocol: Page updates continuously; review EVERY item; capture actual application links. Around 6-9 Aug 2026 search for the September-October edition and switch URLs.

tier1.name: Impact Funding monthly roundup
tier1.protocol: Review every item on the free roundup page; ~250 further items sit on paid-subscriber sector pages - cross-check the e-mail edition on arrival.

tier1.name: Terra Viva Grants - cross-cutting
tier1.protocol: Review every listed item on page 1 (new posts land there).

tier1.name: fundsforNGOs country hub
tier1.protocol: Cycle African country pages via per-country searches (anti-bot blocks direct fetch): fundsforngos [country] deadline [month] 2026.

tier1.name: Coefficient Giving - apply for funding
tier1.protocol: Scan contents sidebar for 'Effective Giving' and 'Abundance & Growth' (and new fund headings); log every open RFP with deadline. Jul 2026: Effective Giving absent; Abundance & Growth live.

tier2_clusters.name: Institutes, bilaterals & implementers
sources: GIZ Africa country pages: Ethiopia, Ghana, Kenya, South Africa, Tanzania (missing tab = no active tenders; full loop is the scraper's job)
DevelopmentAid snippet search: GIZ OR Enabel consultancy tender Africa [month]

un_policy: UN system deprioritised for bidding (heavy process); log UN items only on exceptional fit

rules.rows: org-eligible open items get rows; individual/student/prize-tier items get one report line
rules.links: actual application URL, never a bare domain

report: 'no new matches' is a normal result
